name: Chargebee API
what_it_does: Subscription management and recurring billing platform with support for complex pricing models, revenue recognition (ASC 606/IFRS 15), dunning, trials, and multi-currency billing.
use_cases: Subscription lifecycle management with complex pricing (tiered, per-seat, usage-based)
Automated dunning and payment retry for failed subscription renewals
Revenue recognition automation for SaaS companies
Multi-currency and multi-gateway billing across geographies
Agent-driven subscription upgrades, downgrades, and cancellations
not_for: Simple one-time payment processing (Stripe or PayPal is simpler)
Marketplaces requiring split payments between multiple parties
Early-stage startups where subscription complexity is premature
best_when: An agent manages SaaS subscription billing with complex pricing tiers, usage-based charges, or revenue recognition requirements.
avoid_when: You only need simple one-time payments or your subscription logic is too simple to justify Chargebee's complexity and cost.

auth.notes: HTTP Basic auth with API key as username and empty password. API key is site-specific (test vs live sites are separate subdomains). No scoped keys — all API keys have full site access. Keys managed in dashboard.

pricing.free_tier_limits: 14-day free trial with full feature access; no permanent free tier
pricing.paid_tiers: Performance plan ~$599/month up to $100K MRR; Enterprise custom pricing above that. Starter plan for smaller businesses. Fees are flat monthly (not per-transaction).
pricing.notes: Pricing scales with your MRR, not transaction volume — good for high-volume subscription businesses. More expensive than Stripe Billing for simple use cases but provides dedicated subscription management features.

performance.rate_limits: 100 API calls per second per site by default. Webhook delivery retried for up to 3 days with exponential backoff.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Errors returned with error_type, error_code, and message fields. Error codes are well-documented. Some complex billing scenario errors are verbose but informative. Test site mirrors production API behavior closely.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Idempotency key support via request header on select endpoints. Not uniformly available across all write operations. Agents should use idempotency keys for subscription creation and invoice operations.
known_agent_gotchas: Test and live environments are separate subdomains — agents must switch base URL, not just API key
No API key scoping — a leaked key gives full access to all billing data and operations
Subscription state machine has many states (active, in_trial, non_renewing, paused, cancelled) — agents must handle all transitions
Usage-based billing requires separate usage reporting API calls before invoice generation
Idempotency keys not supported on all endpoints — check docs per-endpoint before relying on them
Webhook event ordering is not guaranteed — idempotent event handlers are required
Large subscription lists require pagination — no cursor-based pagination, only offset which can miss records during concurrent updates

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. HTTP Basic auth with API key is functional but less modern than Bearer tokens. No fine-grained key scoping is a meaningful risk for agents — a compromised key exposes full billing operations. SOC2 Type II and PCI-DSS compliant. EU data residency option available.
